direct_seed.comment: This exact compiler path closes the mainline classical-tail-elision gap.

notes: This artifact allocates versioned leaf values to physical slots using exact live ranges plus same-register overwrite reuse.
It is stricter than the mainline first-reference/last-reference interval approximation and reduces the exact arithmetic-slot peak from 10 named slots to 9 physical slots for the checked-in leaf.

arithmetic_kernel_family.name: litinski_addsub_schoolbook_v1
arithmetic_kernel_family.summary: Imported exact non-Clifford arithmetic kernel family: schoolbook multiplication via controlled add-subtract plus n-1-cost add/sub/select kernels.
arithmetic_kernel_family.exact_scope: exact non-Clifford counts for the named arithmetic-kernel family; Clifford micro-counts are not flattened in this subproject
notes: The multiplier cost follows the controlled add-subtract schoolbook family highlighted by Litinski 2024 for practical low-Toffoli ECC circuits.
This subproject derives arithmetic totals from a named arithmetic-kernel family, but it still does not ship a bit-for-bit primitive CX/CCX expansion for the 256-bit multiplier family.

families.name: folded_linear_scan_tmpand_v1
families.summary: Low-qubit exact lookup family using a 15-bit folded magnitude domain and temporary logical-AND equality ladders.

lowering_strategy.classification: shared folded sign/magnitude decomposition over the checked signed-folded lookup contract
lowering_strategy.lookup_compute: temporary logical-AND equality ladders over the full positive folded domain
lowering_strategy.lookup_uncompute: measurement-reset internal to the temporary logical-AND family

persistent_workspace.name: folded_magnitude_register
persistent_workspace.summary: Stores the shared absolute-value magnitude 0..32767 used by every lookup family.

persistent_workspace.name: lookup_control_predicates
persistent_workspace.summary: Tracks zero/min-word routing and the Y-negation enable predicate without allocating a full metadata table.

stages.name: folded_word_classification
stages.summary: Shared sign/magnitude classification for the 16-bit signed folded contract.

blocks.name: magnitude_all_zero_tree
blocks.summary: Temporary logical-AND ladder over the 15 magnitude bits.
notes: The all-zero predicate is shared by zero-word bypass and 0x8000 special-word routing.
The temporary logical-AND ladder is uncomputed by local measurements inside the stage.

blocks.name: signed_absolute_value_prepare
blocks.summary: Two mirrored temporary logical-AND ladders prepare the folded absolute value for the positive lookup domain.
notes: This stage implements the explicit absolute-value preparation used by all checked lookup families.
Separate zero/min routing is driven from the shared all-zero predicate and the raw sign bit, so no additional non-Clifford flag-materialization block is counted here.
The classification stage is family-independent and implements the same signed-folded contract used by the main repository lookup audit.

stages.name: folded_positive_lookup_compute
stages.summary: Linear scan across the folded positive-magnitude table using temporary logical-AND equality ladders.

blocks.name: magnitude_equality_scan
blocks.summary: One equality ladder for each positive folded-table address.
notes: Every positive-domain table entry 0..32767 is matched coherently against the folded magnitude register.
The ladder family matches the low-space temporary logical-AND approach described in the family name.
The linear-scan family minimizes extra live workspace by reusing a single equality-ladder scratch region across the full positive domain.

stages.name: folded_positive_lookup_uncompute
stages.summary: Measurement-reset is internal to the temporary logical-AND equality scan and does not add non-Clifford cost beyond the compute stage.
notes: The equality ladders are temporary logical-AND blocks whose measurement-reset path is already represented in the compute-stage primitive inventory.

stages.name: conditional_y_negation
stages.summary: Applies the post-lookup Y negation required by negative raw words in the folded contract.

blocks.name: conditional_field_negation
blocks.summary: Field-width conditional subtraction/select network over the Y coordinate.
notes: This stage is the exact post-lookup sign-fix assumption represented as a single field-width conditional negation.
The X coordinate remains unchanged under negation; only the Y coordinate path carries non-Clifford cost.
This remains the lowest-space lookup family checked into the compiler project.
Zero-word and 0x8000 routing are explicit semantic cases, but they do not allocate a separate metadata-table lookup.

families.name: folded_unary_qrom_v1
families.summary: Lower-gate exact lookup family using a full unary conversion register over 32768 folded magnitudes.

lowering_strategy.classification: shared folded sign/magnitude decomposition over the checked signed-folded lookup contract
lowering_strategy.lookup_compute: full unary address decode over the 32768-entry positive folded domain

persistent_workspace.name: folded_magnitude_register
persistent_workspace.summary: Stores the shared absolute-value magnitude 0..32767 used by every lookup family.

persistent_workspace.name: lookup_control_predicates
persistent_workspace.summary: Tracks zero/min-word routing and the Y-negation enable predicate without allocating a full metadata table.

stages.name: folded_word_classification
stages.summary: Shared sign/magnitude classification for the 16-bit signed folded contract.

blocks.name: magnitude_all_zero_tree
blocks.summary: Temporary logical-AND ladder over the 15 magnitude bits.
notes: The all-zero predicate is shared by zero-word bypass and 0x8000 special-word routing.
The temporary logical-AND ladder is uncomputed by local measurements inside the stage.

blocks.name: signed_absolute_value_prepare
blocks.summary: Two mirrored temporary logical-AND ladders prepare the folded absolute value for the positive lookup domain.
notes: This stage implements the explicit absolute-value preparation used by all checked lookup families.
Separate zero/min routing is driven from the shared all-zero predicate and the raw sign bit, so no additional non-Clifford flag-materialization block is counted here.
The classification stage is family-independent and implements the same signed-folded contract used by the main repository lookup audit.

stages.name: folded_positive_lookup_compute
stages.summary: Unary address decode across the positive folded domain.

blocks.name: unary_address_decode
blocks.summary: One coherent unary activation edge for each nonzero folded magnitude transition.
notes: This stage materializes a full unary address register spanning the 32768-entry folded positive domain.
The unary register is the dominant live workspace cost in this family.

stages.name: folded_positive_lookup_uncompute
stages.summary: Exact reverse unary cleanup across the full folded unary address register.

blocks.name: unary_address_uncompute
blocks.summary: Reverse unary QROM cleanup across the full folded positive domain.
notes: This is the exact reverse path of the unary address decode.
The unary workspace remains live for the reverse pass.

stages.name: conditional_y_negation
stages.summary: Applies the post-lookup Y negation required by negative raw words in the folded contract.

blocks.name: conditional_field_negation
blocks.summary: Field-width conditional subtraction/select network over the Y coordinate.
notes: This stage is the exact post-lookup sign-fix assumption represented as a single field-width conditional negation.
The X coordinate remains unchanged under negation; only the Y coordinate path carries non-Clifford cost.
This family minimizes gate count more aggressively than the linear-scan family, but uses a very large unary workspace.
The compute and reverse-uncompute halves are both counted explicitly.

families.name: folded_unary_qrom_measured_uncompute_v1
families.summary: Best exact low-gate family currently checked in: unary folded QROM compute plus 8/7-split measurement-based uncompute.

lowering_strategy.classification: shared folded sign/magnitude decomposition over the checked signed-folded lookup contract
lowering_strategy.lookup_compute: full unary address decode over the 32768-entry positive folded domain

stages.name: folded_positive_lookup_compute
stages.summary: Unary address decode across the positive folded domain.

blocks.name: unary_address_decode

notes: This stage materializes a full unary address register spanning the 32768-entry folded positive domain.

stages.name: folded_positive_lookup_uncompute
stages.summary: Measured 8/7-split cleanup for the unary lookup workspace.

blocks.name: coarse_bucket_measured_uncompute
blocks.summary: Measured cleanup of the coarse 8-bit bucket structure.
notes: The measured cleanup is split into a coarse bucket layer and a fine residual tree.

blocks.name: fine_tree_measured_uncompute
blocks.summary: Measured cleanup of the residual 7-bit tree inside each coarse bucket.
notes: The 8/7 split matches the checked family definition used by the compiler frontier.
The measured cleanup reduces non-Clifford cost relative to the full reverse unary pass.

stages.name: conditional_y_negation
stages.summary: Applies the post-lookup Y negation required by negative raw words in the folded contract.

blocks.name: conditional_field_negation
blocks.summary: Field-width conditional subtraction/select network over the Y coordinate.
notes: This stage is the exact post-lookup sign-fix assumption represented as a single field-width conditional negation.
The X coordinate remains unchanged under negation; only the Y coordinate path carries non-Clifford cost.
This family uses the same forward unary QROM compute as folded_unary_qrom_v1 but replaces reverse uncompute by the exact 8/7-split measurement-based construction.
It improves the exact gate frontier while keeping the lookup semantics identical.
This artifact lowers each named compiler-project lookup family into an explicit stage/block inventory below the contract-only layer.
The lowered families keep the signed-folded secp256k1 lookup semantics exact while exposing stage-local workspace and non-Clifford reconstruction.
This is still not a bit-for-bit primitive qRAM/QROM netlist; it is an explicit compiler-family lowering layer checked below the folded lookup contract.

lookup_families.name: folded_linear_scan_tmpand_v1
lookup_families.summary: Low-qubit exact lookup family using a 15-bit folded magnitude domain and temporary logical-AND equality ladders.
notes: This remains the lowest-space lookup family checked into the compiler project.
Zero-word and 0x8000 routing are explicit semantic cases, but they do not allocate a separate metadata-table lookup.

lookup_families.summary: Lower-gate exact lookup family using a full unary conversion register over 32768 folded magnitudes.
notes: This family minimizes gate count more aggressively than the linear-scan family, but uses a very large unary workspace.
The compute and reverse-uncompute halves are both counted explicitly.

lookup_families.name: folded_unary_qrom_measured_uncompute_v1
lookup_families.summary: Best exact low-gate family currently checked in: unary folded QROM compute plus 8/7-split measurement-based uncompute.
notes: This family uses the same forward unary QROM compute as folded_unary_qrom_v1 but replaces reverse uncompute by the exact 8/7-split measurement-based construction.
It improves the exact gate frontier while keeping the lookup semantics identical.

phase_shell_families.name: full_phase_register_v1
phase_shell_families.summary: Conservative shell that keeps the entire 512-bit phase-estimation register live.
notes: This family mirrors the simplest exact logical accounting: both 256-bit phase registers remain live throughout the oracle inventory.
Rotation synthesis below this shell is still out of scope; the shell affects qubit inventory, not oracle non-Clifford totals.

phase_shell_families.name: semiclassical_qft_v1
phase_shell_families.summary: Space-compressed shell that uses a Griffiths–Niu-style semiclassical inverse QFT and reuses a single phase qubit.
notes: This family treats the phase-estimation shell as a one-qubit live quantum register with classical feed-forward between measurements.

It is the strongest exact qubit-compression lever currently checked into the compiler project because it removes the 512-bit coherent phase-register requirement without changing the oracle family itself.

These are exact whole-oracle counts for named compiler families over a fixed arithmetic-kernel family, an explicit lookup-lowering family, a generated block-inventory layer, and a fully quantum raw-32 schedule.
The qubit frontier uses exact slot allocation and an explicit semiclassical phase-shell option rather than a fixed 512-bit phase-register policy.
The arithmetic kernels remain an imported exact non-Clifford family boundary; whole-oracle counts are exact relative to that family.
